parts: Nestylix arborea, also known as the "tree of life," is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The medicinal parts of the plant are primarily its roots and leaves, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. These compounds have been found to have potential therapeutic applications in treating various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The roots are often used to treat fever and skin conditions, while the leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Nestylix arborea.
